channel.title: Noticias Forex — Global Investing
channel.description: Noticias forex de alto impacto en tiempo real con análisis fundamental para las 8 divisas G8.

item.title: ECB's Sleijpen: Energy prices likely to become entrenched more quickly than in 2022
item.description: European Central Bank policymaker Olaf Sleijpen said on Tuesday that the rising energy prices are likely to become entrenched in wider economy more quickly than they did during the 2022 energy crisis, as reported by Reuters. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: The markets are more settled with the USD higher to kickstart Tuesday's NA session
item.description: Yesterday at this time, markets were in the middle of a dramatic reversal. Earlier declines in equities flipped sharply higher, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average surging more than 1,000 points at its peak. At the same time, yields—which had pushed up toward 4.44%—reversed lower and briefly moved into negative territory on the day. The USD follo... Fuente: InvestingLive

item.title: Gold subdued as mixed US-Iran signals keep investors cautious amid ongoing war
item.description: Gold (XAU/USD) trades with a subdued tone on Tuesday, struggling to build on the previous day’s rebound as investors continue to assess geopolitical developments in the Middle East amid conflicting signals from the United States and Iran over potential negotiations. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: ECB's Sleijpen: Energy prices likely to become more-entrenched in economy than 2022
item.description: Comments from the Dutch central bank President: ECB very vigilant on possible second round effects ECB likely to have more info on second-round effects in April, but data will be limited More than any other central bank, the ECB has made a hawkish pivot in the wake of the energy price spike. That's awfully reminiscent of 2007 and Trichet's missteps... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: The First Federal Reserve Inflation Forecast for March Is In -- and It's Not Pretty
item.description: Key PointsOil price shock events are historically bad news for the Dow Jones Industrial Average, S P 500, and Nasdaq Composite. Fuente: Nasdaq FX

item.title: Euro area: War impact weighs on outlook – Commerzbank
item.description: Commerzbank’s Senior Economist Dr. Vincent Stamer notes that the Euro area composite PMI fell from 51.9 to 50.5 in March, with services weakening and manufacturing flattered by longer delivery times. He links this to the war in Iran, which is hurting expectations and lifting input prices. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: investingLive European FX news wrap: Markets consolidate as focus turns to negotiations
item.description: USDJPY consolidates below the key 160.00 figure as focus turns to US-Iran negotiations UK March flash services PMI 51.2 vs 53.0 expected Trump wants a deal but negotiations not likely to be successful, says Israeli officials Eurozone March flash services PMI 50.1 vs 51.1 expected Germany March flash manufacturing PMI 51.7 vs 49.5 expected France Ma... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: USD/JPY: BoJ wage dynamics back further hikes – BBH
item.description: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad highlights that USD/JPY is trading directionless just below 159.00 as Japanese inflation slowed in February but is expected to rebound. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: GBP: Inflation stalling complicates BoE path – TD Securities
item.description: TD Securities’ Global Strategy Team expects UK inflation to hold at 3.0% year-on-year in February, in line with the Bank of England (BoE) and market consensus. Core CPI is seen steady at 3.1%, with services easing but core goods ticking higher. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: USDJPY consolidates below the key 160.00 figure as focus turns to US-Iran negotiations
item.description: FUNDAMENTAL OVERVIEW USD: The US dollar weakened across the board yesterday after Trump announced on his Truth Social account a five-day ceasefire for negotiations toward “a complete and total resolution of the hostilities.” However, the selloff in the greenback was short-lived as Iran quickly denied the claims. Most notably, Iranian Parliament Spe... Fuente: InvestingLive

item.title: Oro (XAU/USD) Hoy: Señales Técnicas Apuntan a un Fin de la Corrección - 24 marzo 2026
item.description: El oro muestra señales de rebote tras semanas de corrección. Atención a máximos y mínimos recientes, caída del crudo y dólar débil podrían impulsar XAU/USD. Fuente: DailyForex ES

item.title: UK PMIs show manufacturing cost inflation at highest since 1992 Sterling crisis
item.description: UK PMI data for March point to a sharp loss of growth momentum as the Middle East conflict begins to weigh on activity. PMI Manufacturing edged down from 51.7 to 51.4, while PMI Services dropped more notably from 53.9 to 51.2, a six-month low. As a result, PMI Composite declined from 53.7 to 51.0, also […] The post UK PMIs show manufacturing ... Fuente: ActionForex

item.title: UK March flash services PMI 51.2 vs 53.0 expected
item.description: Prior 53.9 Manufacturing PMI 51.4 vs 50.1 expected Prior 51.7 Composite PMI 51.0 vs 52.9 expected Prior 53.7 Key Findings: Input price inflation jumps to its highest for just over three years amid a surge in manufacturing costs Comment: Chris Williamson, Chief Business Economist at S P Global Market Intelligence: “The war in the Middle East has hit... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: Oil: Geopolitics keeps pressure on supply chains – Rabobank
item.description: Rabobank’s Senior Macro Strategist Bas van Geffen notes that Iran’s control over the Strait of Hormuz and ongoing regional tensions are keeping risks elevated for Oil and broader energy markets. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: Trump wants a deal but negotiations not likely to be successful, says Israeli officials
item.description: The Reuters report cites three senior Israeli officials in saying that US president Trump appears to be quite determined in trying to strike a deal with Iran. That as he is aiming to try and end hostilities in the Middle East after the developments in recent weeks. However, the sources say that they are of the view that Iran is not likely to agree ... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: Eurozone March flash services PMI 50.1 vs 51.1 expected
item.description: Prior 51.9 Manufacturing PMI 51.4 vs 49.4 expected Prior 50.8 Composite PMI 50.5 vs 51.0 expected Prior 51.9 The manufacturing sector is the only bright spot, with Germany leading the recovery in that regard. That being said, manufacturing output was softer than what the main index indicates with it being a two-month low. Meanwhile, the drag in the... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: AUD/USD: Constructive path toward 0.75 – OCBC
item.description: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong stay constructive on the Australian Dollar (AUD) despite recent risk-off pressure from higher energy prices. They highlight above-target inflation, resilient domestic activity and a more hawkish Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) as key supports. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bulls target 100-day SMA as hawkish Fed expectations underpin USD
item.description: The USD/CAD pair builds on the previous day's goodish rebound from the 1.3670 area, or a one-week low, and attracts buyers for the second straight day on Tuesday. Spot prices climb to a two-month high during the early part of the European session amid a firmer US Dollar (USD). Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: BoJ: April hike expected after soft data – Danske Bank
item.description: Danske Research Team notes softer Japanese data, with composite PMI slipping and core CPI falling below target for the first time in four years, largely due to fuel subsidies. Despite this, PMIs show rising input prices and a weak Japanese Yen. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: Germany March flash manufacturing PMI 51.7 vs 49.5 expected
item.description: Prior 50.9 Services PMI 51.2 vs 52.5 expected Prior 53.5 Composite PMI 51.9 vs 52.0 expected Prior 53.2 I would ignore the headline figures as they don't reflect the real picture. The commentary is clearly pointing to stagflationary pressures from the US-Iran war and the longer it persists, the worse the negative impact on the economy will be. Key ... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: DXY: Range top holds as headlines steer trade – ING
item.description: ING’s Chris Turner notes the Dollar has softened after comments on possible negotiations with Iran, but sees limited downside while the Strait of Hormuz remains shut and energy prices are elevated. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: BoJ’s Ueda: Inflation to rise moderately, food tax cut impact limited
item.description: BoJ Governor Kazuo Ueda said underlying inflation in Japan is expected to “accelerate moderately”, supported by a tight labor market and evolving corporate pricing behavior. He emphasized that firms are becoming more active in wage and price setting, sustaining a cycle in which wages and prices rise in tandem despite near-term distortio... Fuente: ActionForex

item.title: What are the main events for today?
item.description: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we get the Flash PMIs for the major Eurozone economies and the UK. They are all expected to be worse than the prior month amid the US-Iran war and the disruption in the Strait of Hormuz. We will likely see lower than expected figures across the board. I'm not sure that's going to matter much for the market ... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Tumbles below 0.7000, bearish technical signals emerge
item.description: The AUD/USD pair tumbles to near 0.6980 during the early European session on Tuesday, pressured by escalating tensions in the Middle East. US President Donald Trump said on Monday that he will postpone his deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by five days. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: This oil analysis today at investingLive shows sellers may come out well
item.description: The oil market is currently navigating a period of extreme volatility as geopolitical headlines clash with rapid shifts in diplomatic sentiment. Just a day after crude oil futures plunged over 10% —settling near $88.13 following a temporary postponement of strikes on Iranian energy assets—the narrative has shifted back toward escalation. Fresh repo... Fuente: InvestingLive

item.title: BOJ governor Ueda says underlying inflation is expected to accelerate moderately
item.description: Tight labour market, firmer wages will keep in place cycle in which wages and prices rise in tandem Temporary freeze to food sales tax may briefly push down inflation But it is likely to have a limited impact on inflation expectations as a whole BOJ will guide monetary policy appropriately to stably achieve inflation target accompanied by wage gain... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: Australian inflation data due Wednesday, March 25, 2026. Preview.
item.description: Westpac sees Australia CPI steady but flags energy-driven upside risks. In brief from their preview. Summary: February CPI seen at 0.1% m/m, 3.8% y/y (unchanged) Trimmed mean expected 0.3% m/m, 3.4% y/y Inflation momentum steady but still above target band February data pre-dates Middle East energy shock Fuel prices fell in February, masking underl... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: Japan core CPI falls to 1.7% in February, as energy costs drag inflation lower
item.description: Japan’s inflation slowed further in February, with core CPI (ex-fresh food) easing from 2.0% yoy to 1.6% yoy, below expectations of 1.7% yoy. The drop pushed core inflation below the Bank of Japan’s 2% target for the first time in nearly four years, largely reflecting government measures to curb electricity costs. The decline was driven […] T... Fuente: ActionForex

item.title: Japan PMI composite falls to 52.5, war lifts costs and hits sentiment
item.description: Japan’s private sector growth lost momentum in March as PMI data showed a broad-based slowdown across both manufacturing and services. PMI Manufacturing fell from 53.0 to 51.4, PMI Services eased from 53.8 to 52.8, and PMI Composite declined from 53.9 to 52.5, pointing to softer expansion after a strong start to the year. The slowdown […] The... Fuente: ActionForex

item.title: FX Watch: AUD/CHF and AUD/CAD Bounce Setups for a Strong Australian CPI
item.description: Interest rate hike expectations are running pretty high for the RBA, so a hotter inflation print could seal the deal for May. Here’s what I’m watching on AUD/CHF and AUD/CAD if this happens. Fuente: BabyPips
